titleStmt.title: Letter from Jim Fitzhenry to Bridgit Fitzhenry, 24 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Fitzhenry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from James Fitzhenry (1887-1917) to his sister Bridgit Fitzhenry. He asks her to thank their aunt for a parcel she sent him.
Fitzhenry was born in New Ross, Wexford, He had a sister, Bridgit who was born in c. 1853. He may also have had a brother called Edward who was born in 1883. In the 1911 census he is listed as unmarried, boarding with George and Margaret Woodward and their two sons, at 24 Derlwyn Street, Phillipstown. James is a colliery timberman, working underground. He enlisted in the army in Newport on 3rd September 1914.
His enlistment papers show his address recorded as Glenview Villa, New Tredegar. He became a in the Royal Field Artillery and on 17th May 1915 (no 13140,). He was assigned to the 5th Reserve Brigade, Royal Field Artillery. Through 1915 and into 1916 he was moved to the 4th reserve Battery, 64th Brigade and 65th Brigade until the 30th August 1916 when he was assigned to the 62nd Brigade. He died in 1917 in action.

msIdentifier.collection: The Gunner James Fitzhenry Collection, Wexford County Archive, WXCA/P177/6/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
24/8/16 France My Dear Sister Just a few lines to
let you know I am
in the best of health
few lines
will find you all
right. I had a letter
from K Ahearn a few
days back . She told
me Wins husband is
in Hospital some
where in France
She don't know if she
is coming to Paris or
not. She mentioned some
thing about having been
offered a place. She wishes
to be rembered to you.
Well Dear Sister & home
not much to say at
present. Every thing as
usual. By the way I
received Writing Paper
safe. With thanks for
same & also Auntys letter.
With your Photo . I think
it is a fairly good one
of you. thanks very much
for it. Give my love to
May Cullen when you
write to her — bye the
way I had a letter from
S McCarthy She has had a
letter from May Nolan
and she was asking for
me. I am pleased to hear
she is getting on well.
Tell Bridie I wish to be
rembered to her
We are having fine weather
in so far hope it is
keeping so will you
give my best love to
Aunt & Uncle glad to
hear Uncle is feeling
. I will write to
Aunt before end of week.
Rember me to Jim also
Be good you & God
Bless you all Your loving Brother
Jim xxxx Ps ask May Cullen why
she don't drop me a line
& let me know how she getting on
